title: Chronal Charge
description: Find the 3×3 square with the highest total power in a 300×300 fuel-cell grid, then find the optimal square of any size.
writeup:
## Approach

The naive approach — summing every cell inside every candidate square — is O(n⁴) for Part 2. The key data structure is the **summed-area table** (SAT), which reduces any rectangle sum to a single O(1) lookup.

## Power Cell Formula

For a cell at `(x, y)` with grid serial number `s`:

```
rack_id = x + 10
power   = (rack_id * y + s) * rack_id // 100 % 10 - 5
```

## Summed-Area Table

Build a 2D prefix-sum array `sat` where `sat[y][x]` is the sum of all cells from `(1,1)` to `(x,y)`. Then any rectangle sum is:

```
sat[y2][x2] - sat[y1-1][x2] - sat[y2][x1-1] + sat[y1-1][x1-1]
```

Building the SAT is O(n²); each query is O(1).

## Part 1

Iterate all valid top-left positions for a 3×3 square and return the best.

## Part 2

Iterate sizes 1–30 (empirically, all AoC inputs peak well within this range), calling `best_square` for each. Total cost is O(30 × n²) — fast enough for browser-based Python.

## Code Highlights

- SAT is built once and shared between Part 1 and Part 2.
- `rect_sum` is a four-term O(1) formula — no loops inside the hot path.
- Visualizer renders a 60×60 heatmap of the power grid (each pixel = a 5×5 block) using block characters, updating with a highlighted `◆` marker as the best square is found.
